text: 6 Minute English from bbclearningenglish .com Hello, this is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: 《六分钟英语》来自bbclearningenglish.com。你好,这里是BBC英语教学的《六分钟英语》。

text: I'm Phil.
zh: 我是菲尔。

text: And I'm Pippa.
zh: 我是皮帕。

text: If you've eaten anything today, then it's likely that some of your food was ultra -processed – food containing artificial ingredients like additives and sweeteners.
zh: 如果你今天吃过任何东西,那你的食物里很可能有一些是超加工食品——含有添加剂、甜味剂等人工成分的食物。

text: Ultra -processed foods are are everywhere from sliced bread to chocolate biscuits and crisps.
zh: 超加工食品无处不在,从切片面包到巧克力饼干和薯片。

text: They might taste good, but the bad news is that ultra -processed foods have been linked to poor health.
zh: 它们也许好吃,但坏消息是,超加工食品已被认为与健康不佳有关。

text: They often contain lots of sugar and salt and have been linked to problems like obesity and diabetes.
zh: 它们往往含有大量糖和盐,并被认为与肥胖、糖尿病等问题有关。

text: So how can we tell what food is ultra -processed and what's not?
zh: 那么,我们怎么分辨哪些食物是超加工的、哪些不是呢?

text: Here's health reporter Annabel Rackham on BBC World Service programme What In The World.
zh: 下面是健康记者安娜贝尔·拉克姆在BBC国际频道节目《世界怎么了》中的讲述。

text: Ultra -processed foods are things that contain five or more ingredients and things that you wouldn't find in your average kitchen.
zh: 超加工食品是那种含有五种或更多成分、而且是你普通厨房里找不到的东西。

text: It's going to have things on there like emulsifiers, preservatives, additives, dyes and sweeteners.
zh: 上面会有乳化剂、防腐剂、添加剂、色素和甜味剂之类的东西。

text: Annabel describes ultra -processed foods as things containing ingredients you wouldn't find in your kitchen.
zh: 安娜贝尔把超加工食品描述为含有你厨房里找不到的成分的东西。

text: Do you eat much ultra -processed food, Pippa, or do you try to avoid it?
zh: 你吃很多超加工食品吗,皮帕,还是你尽量避开它?

text: I used to eat a lot of ultra -processed foods and now I try to cook everything myself and not eat things like chocolate and snacks all day.
zh: 我以前吃很多超加工食品,现在我尽量什么都自己做,不再整天吃巧克力和零食之类的东西。

text: How about you?
zh: 你呢?

text: I think the same.
zh: 我觉得我也一样。

text: I try to cook things using just your normal ingredients just so you know what's gone into it.
zh: 我尽量只用普通食材来做东西,这样你就知道里面放了什么。

text: In this episode we'll be discussing ultra -processed food as well as learning some useful new vocabulary.
zh: 本期节目里,我们会讨论超加工食品,同时学一些实用的新词汇。

text: And remember, there's also a quiz and worksheet available on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 记住,我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上还有测验和练习题。

text: But now I have a question for you, Pippa.
zh: 不过现在我有个问题要问你,皮帕。

text: Fizzy drinks like cola and lemonade are another example of popular ultra -processed foods.
zh: 可乐、柠檬汽水这样的碳酸饮料,是另一种受欢迎的超加工食品的例子。

text: But when were fizzy drinks invented?
zh: 但碳酸饮料是什么时候发明的?

text: Well, I think it was before 1972, but 1772 sounds like too early, so I'm going to say B. 1872.
zh: 嗯,我觉得是在1972年之前,但1772年听起来太早了,所以我选B,1872年。

text: We'll find out the answer at the end of the programme.
zh: 我们会在节目结尾揭晓答案。

text: One reason for the popularity of ultra -processed food is convenience.
zh: 超加工食品受欢迎的一个原因是方便。

text: Let's hear more from health reporter Annabel who talks here with Hannah Gelbart, presenter of BBC World Service's What in the World.
zh: 让我们再听听健康记者安娜贝尔的说法,她在这里与BBC国际频道《世界怎么了》主持人汉娜·盖尔巴特交谈。

text: So I do think convenience is the main issue there and again with a ready meal you put it in the microwave for a couple of minutes, it's done, it's hot, it's served to you.
zh: 所以我确实觉得方便是那儿的主要问题,还有,一份即食餐,你放进微波炉转几分钟,就好了,热腾腾地端到你面前。

text: Whereas you know sometimes cooking a fresh meal from scratch that can take a really long time what's your ultra processed guilty food i'm a chocolate girl packet of biscuits something like that a cake
zh: 而你知道,有时从头做一顿新鲜饭菜会花很长时间。你那份让你有负罪感的超加工食品是什么?我是个爱巧克力的姑娘,一包饼干、类似那样的东西,一块蛋糕,

text: that is my that's my guilty pleasure ultra processed foods like ready meals are convenient
zh: 那就是我的、那就是我明知不该却偏爱的东西。像即食餐这样的超加工食品很方便,

text: a ready meal is a meal from a supermarket that has already been prepared and can be heated up quickly in a microwave that's a lot quicker and easier than cooking from scratch
zh: 即食餐是超市里已经做好、可以在微波炉里快速加热的一顿饭,这比从头做饭要快得多、也容易得多。

text: an idiom meaning to do something from the very beginning without using anything that's already been made.
zh: 这个习语意思是从最开头做起,不使用任何已经做好的东西。

text: Even though ultra processed foods are often unhealthy, they taste good.
zh: 尽管超加工食品往往不健康,它们却很好吃。

text: That's why Annabelle calls chocolate her guilty pleasure.
zh: 这就是安娜贝尔把巧克力称作她『明知不该却偏爱之物』的原因。

text: A guilty pleasure is something you enjoy but think you shouldn't and feel a little embarrassed about.
zh: 『guilty pleasure』是你享受、却觉得自己不该、还为此有点难为情的东西。

text: Ultra processed food is a tricky topic.
zh: 超加工食品是个棘手的话题。

text: We know these foods have been linked to poor health but at the same time they're They're cheap, convenient and taste good.
zh: 我们知道这些食物与健康不佳有关,但与此同时,它们又便宜、方便、好吃。

text: So what should we do?
zh: 那我们该怎么办?

text: Here's Hannah and Annabel discussing this for BBC programme What In The World.
zh: 下面是汉娜和安娜贝尔在BBC节目《世界怎么了》中讨论这个问题。

text: Is it OK for me to have a packet of crisps once in a while?
zh: 我偶尔吃一包薯片可以吗?

text: Should we be cutting ultra -processed foods out of our diets completely or is there a way for us to still enjoy them from time to time?
zh: 我们应该把超加工食品完全从饮食中剔除,还是有办法让我们时不时仍能享受它们?

text: I think the best thing to do is just not to panic.
zh: 我觉得最该做的就是别慌。

text: Everything is fine in moderation.
zh: 任何东西适度都没问题。

text: Hannah asks if it's OK to eat ultra -processed foods once in a while or from time to time.
zh: 汉娜问,偶尔或时不时吃点超加工食品行不行。

text: The phrases once in a while and from time to time mean occasionally.
zh: 『once in a while』和『from time to time』这两个短语意思是偶尔。

text: Sometimes, but not very often.
zh: 有时候,但不太频繁。

text: Annabel replies using the phrase everything in moderation, which advises us that it's best to avoid too much of anything.
zh: 安娜贝尔用『everything in moderation』这个短语回答,它提醒我们,任何东西最好都别过量。

text: That sounds sensible to me, and it also means I won't feel bad about eating chocolate now and then.
zh: 这在我听来挺明智的,也意味着我偶尔吃点巧克力时不必内疚。

text: Okay Pippa, it's time to reveal the answer to my question.
zh: 好,皮帕,该揭晓我那道题的答案了。

text: Now I asked you when fizzy drinks were invented.
zh: 我刚问你碳酸饮料是什么时候发明的。

text: You said 1872.
zh: 你说是1872年。

text: I'm afraid the correct answer was 1772.
zh: 很遗憾,正确答案是1772年。

text: Apparently carbonated water was used to try to prevent scurvy on sea voyages.
zh: 据说,当年人们用碳酸水来试图预防海上航行中的坏血病。

text: Wow that is amazing.
zh: 哇,那太神奇了。

text: I wouldn't have thought it was that long ago.
zh: 我想不到会有那么久远。

text: Right, let's recap the vocabulary we've learned, starting with ready meal.
zh: 好,我们来回顾一下学到的词汇,先从『ready meal』(即食餐)开始。

text: A meal from a supermarket that has already been prepared so you can heat it up quickly.
zh: 超市里已经做好、你可以快速加热的一顿饭。

text: If you do something from scratch, you do it from the very beginning without using anything that's already been made.
zh: 如果你『from scratch』做某事,就是从最开头做起,不使用任何已经做好的东西。

text: A guilty pleasure is something you enjoy but feel guilty or embarrassed about because you think you shouldn't do it.
zh: 『guilty pleasure』是你享受、却因为觉得不该做而内疚或难为情的东西。

text: The phrases once in a while and from time to time mean occasionally, not very often.
zh: 『once in a while』和『from time to time』这两个短语意思是偶尔,不太频繁。

text: And finally, the phrase everything in moderation is used to advise someone that it's best to avoid too much of anything.
zh: 最后,『everything in moderation』这个短语用来提醒某人,任何东西最好都别过量。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up, but if you're hungry for more, head over to our website bbclearningenglish .com for more tasty topics and useful vocabulary.
zh: 又一次,我们的六分钟到了,但如果你意犹未尽,就到我们网站bbclearningenglish.com来,看更多美味的话题和实用词汇。

text: See you again soon, but for now, it's goodbye.
zh: 很快再见,不过现在,要说再见了。

text: Bye!
zh: 拜拜!

text: 6 Minute English, from bbclearningenglish .com
zh: 《六分钟英语》,来自bbclearningenglish.com。
